agents-md.name: AGENTS.md
agents-md.evidence: Cross-tool convention documented at agents.md and read by multiple coding agents, with public examples from OpenAI Codex and Apache Airflow.
agents-md.read_by: Most coding agents - OpenAI Codex, Cursor, Jules, Aider, Gemini CLI, Zed, and others
agents-md.location: Repository root; the nearest AGENTS.md up the tree wins, and nested files override parents
agents-md.summary: A plain-Markdown "README for agents" - build/test commands, conventions, and gotchas an agent needs before touching the code. The most widely adopted cross-tool instruction file.

claude-md.name: CLAUDE.md
claude-md.evidence: Official Claude Code memory docs define CLAUDE.md loading locations and behavior; public examples appear in multiple active repos.
claude-md.read_by: Claude Code, and tools that read the Claude memory convention
claude-md.location: Repo root or .claude/CLAUDE.md, ancestor/subdirectory CLAUDE.md files, ~/.claude/ (user-global), enterprise-managed policy paths, .claude/rules/*.md, and CLAUDE.local.md (personal)
claude-md.summary: Anthropic's memory file for Claude Code - loaded automatically at session start to carry project commands, style rules, and standing instructions across turns.

tool-specific-instructions.name: Tool-specific instruction files
tool-specific-instructions.evidence: Gemini, Qwen, Warp, Amp, Aider, and Copilot each document or ship named instruction files, while several now bridge to AGENTS.md.

files.name: GEMINI.md
files.note: Google Gemini CLI.

files.name: AGENT.md
files.note: Amp (Sourcegraph) - legacy singular form; Amp now reads AGENTS.md (plural).

files.name: QWEN.md
files.note: Qwen Code.

files.name: WARP.md
files.note: Warp terminal - backwards-compatible with project rules; Warp now defaults new projects to AGENTS.md, but WARP.md still takes same-directory priority.

files.name: CONVENTIONS.md
files.note: Aider - opt-in: loaded via --read CONVENTIONS.md or a .aider.conf.yml entry, not auto-read like AGENTS.md.

files.name: copilot-instructions.md
files.note: GitHub Copilot (lives under .github/).

tool-specific-instructions.read_by: Each file is read by its namesake tool - Gemini CLI, Amp, Qwen Code, Warp, Aider, GitHub Copilot - often alongside or as a bridge to AGENTS.md
tool-specific-instructions.summary: Per-tool instruction files that predate or coexist with AGENTS.md. Some tools now default to AGENTS.md while keeping legacy filenames alive, so these variants still matter when auditing real repositories.

memory-md.name: MEMORY.md
memory-md.evidence: Claude Code auto-memory docs describe MEMORY.md as the per-project memory index loaded every session, with public examples of the pattern.
memory-md.read_by: Claude Code's auto-memory - the per-project MEMORY.md index it writes and re-reads each session
memory-md.location: Claude Code: ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/MEMORY.md (the index), with detail offloaded to topic files beside it
memory-md.summary: A persistent, agent-maintained index of durable facts - written and re-read across sessions so an agent accumulates project memory instead of relearning each time.

memory-bank.name: Memory Bank
memory-bank.evidence: Cline documents Memory Bank as a structured multi-file context system, and public repositories commit the full file set.

files.name: projectbrief.md
files.note: Foundation doc - why the project exists, core requirements and goals.

files.name: productContext.md
files.note: Problem space and the intended user experience.

files.name: activeContext.md
files.note: Current focus, recent changes, next steps - the most frequently updated file.

files.name: systemPatterns.md
files.note: Architecture, key technical decisions, and design patterns.

files.name: techContext.md
files.note: Technologies, setup, constraints, and dependencies.

files.name: progress.md
files.note: What works, what's left to build, and known issues.

memory-bank.read_by: Cline, Roo Code, and Cursor (via the Memory Bank custom-instructions pattern)
memory-bank.location: A memory-bank/ directory at the repository root
memory-bank.summary: Cline's structured memory system - a set of Markdown files an agent reads at the start of every task to reconstruct full project context after its session memory resets. The six files shown are Cline's set; tools like Roo Code use an overlapping but different variant.

spec-kit.name: Spec Kit
spec-kit.evidence: GitHub Spec Kit publishes templates and slash-command workflows that consume constitution, spec, plan, and tasks files.

files.name: constitution.md
files.note: Project principles and non-negotiable rules the agent honors across every feature.

files.name: spec.md
files.note: The what and why of one feature - requirements and user stories, no implementation detail.

files.name: plan.md
files.note: The technical approach, stack, and architecture for the spec.

files.name: tasks.md
files.note: An ordered, reviewable task list generated from the plan.

spec-kit.read_by: GitHub Spec Kit's slash-command agents (Copilot, Claude, Gemini, Cursor, and more)
spec-kit.summary: GitHub's spec-driven workflow - a constitution plus per-feature spec → plan → tasks files that drive an agent through structured, reviewable implementation.

kiro-steering.name: Kiro steering files
kiro-steering.evidence: Kiro docs define .kiro/steering files and public Kiro projects commit the standard product, structure, and tech files.

files.name: product.md
files.note: What the product is and who it's for.

files.name: structure.md
files.note: File organization and architectural conventions.

files.name: tech.md
files.note: Allowed frameworks, libraries, and technical constraints.

kiro-steering.read_by: AWS Kiro (steering files are largely Kiro-specific)
kiro-steering.summary: Kiro's always-on steering docs - product, structure, and tech files that give the agent persistent project context outside of any single spec.

skill-md.name: SKILL.md
skill-md.evidence: Agent Skills documentation and public skill repositories define SKILL.md as the load-on-demand capability manifest.
skill-md.read_by: Claude (Agent Skills), Claude Code, and the open Agent Skills ecosystem
skill-md.location: A skill directory: <skill-name>/SKILL.md (plus bundled scripts and resources)
skill-md.summary: A self-contained, model-invoked capability: YAML frontmatter (name + description) tells the agent when to load it; the body teaches it how. Progressive disclosure keeps it cheap until needed.

prompt-assets.name: Prompt asset files
prompt-assets.evidence: Prompty, Genkit, and application repos publish external prompt files that tools load independently of source code.

files.name: .prompty
files.note: Prompty - YAML frontmatter (model + inputs) over a templated prompt body.

files.name: .prompt
files.note: Generic templated prompt file (Genkit dotprompt and similar).

files.name: system_prompt.txt
files.note: A plain-text externalized system prompt.

prompt-assets.read_by: Prompty tooling, Azure AI / Semantic Kernel, and apps that load externalized prompts
prompt-assets.summary: Externalized prompt files - Prompty's YAML-front-mattered .prompty, plain .prompt templates, and system_prompt.txt - that pull the prompt out of source code so it can be versioned and edited on its own. Only .prompty has a formal spec (prompty.ai); .prompt and system_prompt.txt are ad-hoc externalized-prompt filenames.

claude-commands.name: Claude Code commands
claude-commands.evidence: Claude Code slash-command docs define .claude/commands/*.md, and active repos commit reusable command files.

files.name: .md
files.note: Any Markdown file under .claude/commands/; the filename is the command name (pr.md -> /pr). Optional YAML frontmatter: description, argument-hint, allowed-tools, model.

claude-commands.summary: A Markdown file Claude Code exposes as a /slash-command - a reusable, version-controlled prompt workflow, with optional frontmatter (allowed-tools, model, argument-hint) and $ARGUMENTS and shell placeholders (@file references are a general Claude Code prompt feature, not command-specific). Now converging with Agent Skills, but still widely committed in its own right.

copilot-prompt-files.name: Copilot prompt & instruction files
copilot-prompt-files.evidence: VS Code Copilot docs define .github/prompts/*.prompt.md and .github/instructions/*.instructions.md, with public examples in GitHub repos.

files.name: .prompt.md
files.note: Invokable saved prompts under .github/prompts/, run as a slash-command.

files.name: .instructions.md
files.note: Auto-attached instructions under .github/instructions/, scoped by an applyTo glob in frontmatter.

copilot-prompt-files.read_by: GitHub Copilot in VS Code / Copilot CLI
copilot-prompt-files.summary: Modular, path-scoped Copilot context: *.instructions.md auto-attach to matching files via an applyTo glob, while *.prompt.md are reusable prompts you invoke by name - the granular cousins of a single .github/copilot-instructions.md.

mcp-config.name: MCP server config
mcp-config.evidence: MCP hosts including Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Cursor, and VS Code document project or app-level MCP server config files.

files.name: .mcp.json
files.note: Claude Code project-scoped MCP servers. The same { "mcpServers": {...} } schema is reused under different filenames: .cursor/mcp.json (Cursor), .vscode/mcp.json (VS Code / Copilot), and claude_desktop_config.json (Claude Desktop).

mcp-config.read_by: Claude Code, Cursor, VS Code / Copilot, and Claude Desktop - every MCP host reads the same mcpServers schema, though the filename and path differ per tool
mcp-config.location: Per tool: .mcp.json (Claude Code repo root), .cursor/mcp.json (Cursor), .vscode/mcp.json (VS Code / Copilot), or the app config dir (claude_desktop_config.json)
mcp-config.summary: A JSON file that tells an agent which Model Context Protocol servers to launch and how (command, args, env) - making a project's tool and data integrations portable, shareable, and version-controlled across every MCP-capable client.

agent-rules.name: Rules files
agent-rules.evidence: Cursor, Cline, and Windsurf document and/or detect project rule files, including both legacy single files and directory-based rules.

files.name: .cursorrules
files.note: Cursor - legacy single-file form (superseded by .cursor/rules/*.mdc).

files.name: .mdc
files.note: Cursor - modern rule file with frontmatter and glob scoping, under .cursor/rules/.

files.name: .clinerules
files.note: Cline - legacy single-file form, still detected.

files.name: .clinerules/
files.note: Cline - primary workspace rules directory; Markdown/text files are combined and can be toggled.
files.pattern: A project-root directory containing focused Markdown or text rule files such as `coding.md`, `testing.md`, and `architecture.md`. Cline combines the files into one workspace rule set, while the UI can toggle individual rules.

files.name: .windsurfrules
files.note: Windsurf - legacy single-file form (superseded by .windsurf/rules/*.md).

agent-rules.location: Repo root, or a rules directory (.cursor/rules/*.mdc, .clinerules/, .windsurf/rules/*.md)
agent-rules.summary: Per-tool rule files that scope agent behavior - older single-file forms (.cursorrules, .clinerules, .windsurfrules) and newer directory-based, glob-scoped forms (.cursor/rules/*.mdc, .clinerules/, .windsurf/rules/*.md).

ai-ignore-files.name: AI ignore files
ai-ignore-files.evidence: Cursor, JetBrains Junie, and Codeium/Windsurf-style tools use gitignore-like files to exclude paths from AI context.

files.name: .aiignore
files.note: JetBrains Junie / AI Assistant.

files.name: .cursorignore
files.note: Cursor.

files.name: .codeiumignore
files.note: Codeium (now Windsurf).

files.name: .aiexclude
files.note: Android Studio Gemini / JetBrains AI exclusion file.

ai-ignore-files.summary: gitignore-syntax files that fence an AI agent out of paths - secrets, vendored code, generated output - so they're never sent to the model as context.

design-md.name: DESIGN.md
design-md.evidence: Google Labs published design.md as a machine-readable design spec, and public design-md catalogs contain real examples.
design-md.read_by: Google Stitch natively; and coding agents (e.g. Claude Code) when pointed at it as design context
design-md.location: Repository root or a design/ directory
design-md.summary: A structured, machine-readable design specification - tokens, components, and layout intent - that an agent reads to generate or keep UI consistent with an established system. Open-sourced by Google Labs in 2026 as a cross-tool draft spec.

llms-txt.name: llms.txt
llms-txt.evidence: llms.txt is published by many developer-docs sites, including Anthropic and Stripe, even though major-provider consumption is unconfirmed.

files.name: llms.txt
files.note: Curated index: short description plus linked sections of the key docs.

files.name: llms-full.txt
files.note: The full docs concatenated into one file for direct ingestion.
files.pattern: Every documentation page concatenated into a single plain-text file for direct ingestion - routinely multiple megabytes. Structurally it's `llms.txt` with each linked page inlined in full; nothing instance-specific is worth vendoring, so this entry links live instances rather than copying a dump.

llms-txt.read_by: Docs sites publish it for LLM tools and crawlers - though no major provider has confirmed reading it
llms-txt.summary: A proposed-turned-widely-published standard: a root-level Markdown file giving LLMs a curated, link-rich map of a site's docs. Published across hundreds of developer-docs sites - though whether the major LLM providers actually read it remains unproven.

pricing-md.name: pricing.md
pricing-md.evidence: Multiple production SaaS sites expose Markdown pricing pages at predictable .md URLs for agent-friendly retrieval.
pricing-md.read_by: Agents and LLM browsers fetching a clean, parse-able pricing page
pricing-md.location: Site path mirroring the HTML page: /pricing.md
pricing-md.summary: The Markdown twin of a pricing page - same URL with a .md suffix - so an agent gets structured plans and numbers instead of scraping marketing HTML. A concrete, shipping instance of the page.md pattern.

auth-md.name: auth.md
auth-md.evidence: WorkOS publishes both the auth.md spec page and a live auth.md file, but adoption beyond that ecosystem remains early.
auth-md.read_by: Agents discovering how to authenticate to a service (early adopters)
auth-md.summary: A Markdown file that tells an agent how to authenticate with a service - discovery of auth endpoints and flows. Shipped by WorkOS as a real, working convention, but adoption beyond it is still early.

ai-txt.name: ai.txt
ai-txt.evidence: Spawning popularized deployed ai.txt files and a 2026 Internet-Draft proposes /.well-known/ai.txt, but crawler obedience and adoption remain unproven.
ai-txt.read_by: AI training/data-mining crawlers that voluntarily honor AI usage preferences; crawler support is not yet reliable
ai-txt.location: Site root /ai.txt in Spawning's deployed pattern; /.well-known/ai.txt in the newer Internet-Draft
ai-txt.summary: A text file declaring machine-readable consent, licensing, or policy preferences for AI training and data-mining. Spawning popularized the deployed root-file pattern, and a 2026 Internet-Draft now proposes a well-known URI; adoption and crawler obedience are still thin, so it stays 🔵.

agent-cards.name: Agent Cards (A2A)
agent-cards.evidence: A2A v1.0 specifies Agent Cards and the well-known agent-card.json path, with public sample cards but still-early ecosystem adoption.

files.name: agent-card.json
files.note: Current A2A well-known Agent Card filename.

files.name: agent.json
files.note: Legacy alias seen in earlier A2A material and deployments.
files.pattern: A legacy well-known alias for an A2A Agent Card. New agents should publish `/.well-known/agent-card.json`; clients may still tolerate `/.well-known/agent.json` while interoperating with older deployments.

agent-cards.read_by: A2A-compatible agents discovering another agent's capabilities
agent-cards.summary: The Agent2Agent (A2A) capability card - a JSON document at a well-known path advertising an agent's skills, endpoints, and auth so other agents can discover and call it. Now a Linux Foundation project at v1.0; adoption is growing but early.

protocols-md.name: The protocols.md namespace
protocols-md.evidence: protocols.md is a published namespace of aspirational .md protocols, but there are no demonstrated readers or broad deployments.

files.name: proof.md
files.note: The namespace's flagship - live at its own apex domain (proof.md → www.proof.md), a v1.0 'living specification' that self-describes as 'an experiment.'

protocols-md.location: Per-domain pages under the protocols.md namespace (e.g. proof.md)
protocols-md.summary: A single maintainer's pre-registered namespace of ~74 aspirational .md "protocols" (proof.md, signature.md, reputation.md, …) staked as Schelling points for a future agent web. Published concept, no demonstrated adoption - see the page for the audited, honest caveats.
